Amended Notice Of Motion To Vacate Arbitration Order, Impose Sanctions, Declare Fairclaims An Unfair Forum, And Void Turo?S Arbitration Clause, Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Patrick Blake, Esq., Blake Law Firm P.C. 619-509-4430 blake@blakelawfirm.com, Attorney for Plaintiff, Patrick Blake (via ZOOM)
Sarah A. Marsey, Esq., HOLLAND KNIGHT LLP 415.743.6900 sarah.marsey@hklaw.com Attorney for Defendant Turo Inc.

Hearing is held. Discussed, Not ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF PATRICK BLAKE'S Motion To Vacate Arbitration Order, Impose Sanctions, Declare Fairclaims An Unfair Forum, And Void Turo's Arbitration Clause

For the reasons stated herein, plaintiff Patrick Blakes motion to vacate arbitration order and award additional relief is denied.

(see word minutes for completed details)

Order is signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Feb-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff PATRICK BLAKE, in pro per present via Zoom videoconference (tel: 619--509-4430).
Atty. SARAH MARSEY, Esq. of Holland and Knight LLP (tel: 415-743-6910) for Defendant Turo Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT TURO INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

Matter argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court takes the matter under submission. The Court stays discovery pending a decision on defendant's motion to compel arbitration.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)
